title: Add chat command deny list for LLM-dispatched commands
description:
SECURITY FINDING (Council Seat 7): The chat command intent path dispatches LLM-generated commands with only a StartsWith('/') check. No deny list exists for destructive commands. The LLM is explicitly told "You CAN execute Minecraft server commands (/give, /tp, /setblock, /time, etc.)" with no restriction on dangerous commands.

Add:
1. A deny list of prohibited server commands (/op, /deop, /kill, /ban, /pardon, /stop, /save-off, /difficulty, /gamerule, /setworldspawn, etc.)
2. All denied commands should be blocked even when CommandExecutionEnabled is true
3. Log every dispatched command at Warning level with full context (goal, intent, timestamp)
4. Consider a separate AllowDestructiveCommands toggle for commands that modify server state beyond item provisioning

Referenced in: Data/Pages/council/2026-06-30-external-audits-council.md

comments.author: anonymous
comments.body: Created by SteveBot council review. Security finding — no deny list on LLM-dispatched chat commands. LLM hallucination could dispatch /op, /kill @e, /ban, etc. Needs deny list + Warning-level audit logging. See council report.
